headline: Amazon AWS AI first agent baseline. Parent-entity Amazon labor-rights structural change: March 31 2026 NLRB settlement (1,300 facilities, retaliation-against-strikers cessation); April 3 2026 NLRB ruling — first Teamsters bargaining order against Amazon (Staten Island ALU-IBT Local 1); concurrent Amazon constitutional challenge to NLRB itself. Pentagon AI cohort participation (May 1 2026, classified military-operations agreements). Published composite 33.9 / Developing band reflects pre-NLRB-ruling baseline; the March-April 2026 NLRB rulings produce mixed signal — compliance with cease-and-desist orders is institutional-acknowledgment positive, but constitutional challenge to NLRB jurisdiction is INT-erosive (declared labor-rights commitments contradicted by structural challenge). Pentagon classified-cohort participation is BND-erosive. Net composite drift: ~-1.5 points; sub-threshold. Recommend confirm at 33.9 with first-baseline rationale.
key_evidence: March 31 2026 NLRB settlement: Amazon agreed to stop retaliating against striking workers; covers 1,300 facilities nationwide.
April 3 2026 NLRB ruling: Amazon must negotiate with Staten Island ALU-IBT Local 1 — first Teamsters bargaining order against Amazon.
Amazon argues NLRB is unconstitutional concurrent with subjection to federal court orders to cease firing employees for protected labor activities.
Pentagon AI cohort participation (May 1 2026): classified military-operations agreements alongside Palantir and 5 others.
AWS / Amazon AI integration with Department of Defense and intelligence community contracts.
Compass Datacenters / hyperscaler labor practices and contractor labor conditions documented.

evidence.finding: First Teamsters bargaining order against Amazon (Staten Island).
Federal court order requires Amazon to cease firing employees for protected labor activities.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 confirmed. Workplace-injury reporting and ESG disclosure functional but reactive; OSHA / NLRB actions surface harm rather than internal mechanisms.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.0 confirmed. Documented warehouse-labor productivity demands and injury-rate patterns sustain critical-band-proximate placement.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.0 confirmed. Operational scale and resource mobilization capacity strong; deployment of safety improvements reactive but substantial.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.0 confirmed. Multiple federal-court / NLRB orders demonstrate sustained boundary erosion; March 2026 settlement and April bargaining order represent enforced compliance rather than voluntary recognition.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 confirmed. ESG and sustainability disclosure functional; NLRB-imposed accountability rather than self-initiated.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.0 confirmed. Declared 'best place to work' commitment contradicted by sustained NLRB findings; concurrent constitutional challenge to NLRB itself is INT-erosive but does not yet drop the dimension below the published anchor.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction yields 35.9 vs published 33.9. Discrepancy +2.0. Math-hygiene flag for data-team review per cluster protocol; no composite change proposed in evidence cycle. Dimension means anchored to evidence; composite-reconstruction is data-team task.

math_hygiene_flag.issue: Published composite 33.9 vs reconstructed 35.9 from dimension means. Discrepancy +2.0 points.
math_hygiene_flag.action: Flag for data-team review per math-hygiene cluster protocol. Do NOT propose score change in evidence cycle.
math_hygiene_flag.evidence_basis_for_dimensions: Dimension means as published are consistent with evidence base; minor compression in composite reconstruction parallels open math-hygiene cluster pattern.

negativeSignals: NLRB-imposed compliance rather than voluntary recognition; constitutional challenge to NLRB itself; Pentagon classified-cohort participation; warehouse labor productivity / injury-rate documentation; persistent retaliation findings.
positiveSignals: March 31 2026 settlement covering 1,300 facilities; April 3 2026 first Teamsters bargaining order — compliance-track outcomes; large-scale climate pledges; accessibility / Alexa-based service for disabled communities; cloud infrastructure for nonprofit / public-sector workloads.
sector_context: AI Labs / hyperscaler labor cluster. Cross-reference: Microsoft (Pentagon-cohort overlap, Nadella testimony pending), Palantir (program-of-record floor entity), Google (DeepMind separate). Amazon distinguished by scale of NLRB action footprint (1,300 facilities) and concurrent constitutional-challenge posture.
open_actions_resolved: Math-hygiene flag for data-team review (+2.0 discrepancy)
open_actions_carried: Watch: Staten Island ALU-IBT Local 1 first-contract negotiation progress
Watch: any further Pentagon classified-cohort scope expansion
